2606.25719.title: Position Spaces and Graphs
2606.25719.paper_summary: In this paper, we introduce position graphs, a graph-based reasoning framework based on the formalization of position spaces. This framework utilizes two strict partial orders, representing horizontal and vertical alignment and precedence, to model the relative positions of discrete tokens. Unlike general qualitative spatial calculi, position graphs are constrained by a chain condition and compatibility requirements that focus on rows and columns. We provide a comprehensive theoretical analysis of this representation, beginning with a characterization of graph consistency. Conditions to ensure the consistency of position graphs are established. Furthermore, we investigate the computational complexity of structural pattern discovery, modeled as the induced subgraph isomorphism problem. We demonstrate that this problem remains NP-complete even within the restricted class of position graphs. While initially motivated by document processing, this work focuses on the underlying mathematical properties and algebraic consistency of position-based constraints, providing a formal logical layer that is independent of specific data extraction techniques.
2606.25719.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們介紹了位置圖，這是一種基於位置空間形式化的圖形推理框架。<lb/>這個框架利用兩個嚴格的偏序，分別表示水平和垂直的對齊及優先順序，以建模離散標記的相對位置。<lb/>與一般的定性空間計算不同，位置圖受到鏈條條件和兼容性要求的限制，這些要求專注於行和列。<lb/>我們對這種表示法進行了全面的理論分析，首先對圖的一致性進行了特徵描述。<lb/>確保位置圖一致性的條件已經建立。<lb/>此外，我們還研究了結構模式發現的計算複雜性，將其建模為誘導子圖同構問題。<lb/>我們證明即使在位置圖的限制類別中，這個問題仍然是 NP 完全的。<lb/>雖然最初受到文檔處理的啟發，但這項工作專注於基於位置的約束的基本數學性質和代數一致性，提供了一個獨立於特定數據提取技術的形式邏輯層。
2606.25719.author: Rita-Nathalia Assaf et.al.
2606.25719.authors: Rita-Nathalia Assaf, Tom Davot, Frédéric Lardeux, Frédéric Saubion
